sections.paragraph:
fut. יִגֹּשׂ (once יִנְגֹּושׂ Isa 58:3), [“Arabic نجش”] to impel, to urge, to drive-

(1) a labourer to work, Isa 58:3. Whence part. נֹוגֵשׂ a taskmaster, ἐργοδιώκτης, Exo 3:7 Job 3:18 also followed by בְּ, hence נֹגֵשׂ בֹּו Isa 9:3 used of a driver of animals, Job 39:7.

(2) to urge a debtor, to demand a debt, with an acc. of pers. Deu 15:2, Deu 15:3to demand tribute, followed by two acc. of the tribute and those on whom it is levied, 2Ki 23:35 part. נֹוגֵשׂ an exactor of tribute, Dan 11:20.

(3) to reign, to rule, part. נֹוגֵשׂ a ruler, a tyrant, Isa 3:12 14:2 60:17 Zec 10:4. Ethiop. ነገሠ፡ id. whence ንጉሥ፡ and ነጋሢ፡ a king; ንጉሠ፡ ነገሥት፡ king of kings, a title of the king of Ethiopia.

Niphal נִגַּשׂ to be pressed, harassed, 1Sa 13:6 Isa 53:7 to press or harass one another, Isa 3:5 to be harassed with toil, to be wearied out (used of an army), 1Sa 14:24.
